data.body:
COMPLETION VERIFICATION (read-only, fresh session) — VERDICT: FAIL (blocks close).

Scope: epic c81c (sulky-pane-flag), "plan-review container fan-out (G3/G4)" optimization. Verified each child's COMPILED state this session via show_ticket against the live tree.

PRIMARY ACCEPTANCE CRITERION UNMET — "All six child stories (S1-S6) closed."
- S1 0250-36d4-c97f-47b4 (make-fawn-box, runner-seam caching): status=in_progress, signature=null.
- S2 c6e5-9936-d07c-4b16 (pale-fist-prow, engine-wide caching): status=in_progress, signature=null.
- S3 ba7e-dd8d-7cac-453f (vale-noun-tumor, parallelize + warm-then-fan-out + budget cap): status=in_progress, signature=null.
- S4 98c6-81c7-175e-416f (run-atom-drawl, merge G3+G4): status=OPEN, signature=null.
- S5 1762-9641-993a-464f (adept-pen-flame, child bin-packing): status=OPEN, signature=null.
- S6 da34-ac98-e375-4af0 (safe-peg-tuner, fidelity-eval harness): status=in_progress, signature=null.
=> 0 of 6 children closed (2 open, 4 in_progress); none carries a completion-verifier signature. The epic itself is status=in_progress (preconditions_summary.status="pre-manifest").

OTHER ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA — NOT DEMONSTRABLE AS CLOSED DELIVERABLES:
- "8-child epic review completes in minutes (was ~27 min) with materially lower token cost (cache reads non-zero)": no epic-level end-to-end measurement is recorded as a closed deliverable; the S1/S3 de-risk runs (E1/E2/A) are SPIKES, not the epic acceptance measurement. The eval-gated children S4/S5 are unstarted (open).
- "Findings remain within offline-eval tolerance (no fidelity regression)": the fidelity instrument is S6's live semantic eval (in_progress); S4/S5 are explicitly eval-gated on S6 (da34 blocks 98c6 and 1762) and remain open. Two live, unresolved S4 container-review G4 findings (per-child guardrail ownership split between S4 and S5) and the S6 G3/G4 findings (AC4 "offline-eval tolerance" vs the live non-blocking eval; S4/S5-vs-S6 gate-strength mismatch) are still open on the children.
- "make lint + full suite pass": not verifiable as an epic-close artifact while the eval-gated work (S4/S5) is unimplemented.

NOTE (does not change the verdict): substantial S1/S3/S6 code HAS landed (runner.py:421 _anthropic_cache_settings + anthropic gating; pass1.py warm-then-fan-out incl. CACHE_MIN_PREFIX_TOKENS / _CONTAINER_MAX_WORKERS; src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-container.eval.yaml present). But completion is determined by signed child-ticket CLOSURE, not code presence; closure status is authoritative and shows the work incomplete. Note also S2/S3/S6 were FORCE_CLAIMed (plan-review gate bypassed by user approval), so even the in_progress claims lack a verified plan-review attestation.

CONCLUSION: The epic does not meet its completion requirements. Closing it now would require --force-close (leaving it unsigned — the durable signal that validation did not pass). Path to close: drive S1/S2/S3/S6 to signed close, implement+close the eval-gated S4/S5 (resolving the open S4/S6 G4 findings), then re-verify the epic-level wall-clock/token/fidelity/lint criteria.
